description_long: This object is a cluster of three mushrooms with thick, robust stems and rounded caps. The caps are a pale yellowish-brown color with some darker spots and a slightly uneven, matte surface. The stems are thick, white to grayish, and covered in a rough, patchy texture with some dark brown areas near the base, suggesting soil or organic debris. The mushrooms are fused at the base, with one smaller mushroom growing alongside two larger ones. The caps are bulbous and slightly asymmetrical, with no visible gills from any angle. The overall appearance is natural and somewhat rugged, resembling wild mushrooms that might be found in a park or urban green space.
description: A cluster of three thick-stemmed, pale yellow-brown capped mushrooms with rough, patchy white stems and no visible gills.
description_view_0: One large mushroom cap is prominent, with a thick, curved stem and rough, patchy surface visible.
description_view_1: Two large mushroom caps and a smaller one are visible, showing the cluster formation and the rough, mottled stems.
description_view_2: The two main caps are seen from an angle, with the smaller mushroom at the base and the rough, soil-stained stems.
description_view_3: Both large caps are clearly visible, with the thick, fused stems and dark, rough base exposed.
